# Summary

This book has walked through the full course-production workflow:

- setting up the authoring environment
- managing course materials with Git and GitHub
- writing reproducible documents with Quarto
- designing the course feeder workbook
- building a Quarto microsite with a consistent `M01_*` module structure
- using GitHub Classroom for code-based coursework
- preparing content for Blackboard Ultra
- automating repetitive course-building tasks with scripts and AI-assisted tools

The goal is not just to create one attractive course website. The goal is to give
professors a repeatable system they can reuse across semesters, sections, and
courses.

The recommended next step is to build a small pilot course using the same
structure: one syllabus page, one schedule page, one deliverables page, and one
complete module folder. Once that pilot renders cleanly and maps into Blackboard
Ultra, the same pattern can scale to the full course.
